ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03311171 covers milk tea as a category; no specific entry exists for wildflower honey green milk tea. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific code exists for honey green milk tea. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34111008 represents tea as a substance; used as closest parent since no specific milk tea or honey green tea code exists.

serving.description: 1 medium cup (approximately 500 ml / 500 g as served with ice)
serving.natural_language_portion: One medium cup of wildflower honey green milk tea as served at KOI Thé, approximately 500 ml, containing brewed green tea, fresh milk, and wildflower honey over ice.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, tea, green, brewed, regular (USDA SR Legacy 14355) and Beverages, milk, whole (USDA SR Legacy 01077) and Honey (USDA SR Legacy 19296); composite estimation for green milk tea with honey
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for wildflower honey green milk tea exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy data for brewed green tea (FDC 14355), whole milk (FDC 01077), and honey (FDC 19296) in proportions consistent with a typical Taiwanese-style green milk tea: approximately 55% brewed green tea, 35% whole milk, and 10% honey by volume, then normalised to 100 g. Energy calculated from macros using Atwater factors. This is a medium-confidence estimate; values should be verified against a laboratory analysis of the KOI Thé product if available.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — honey GI ~58; milk GI ~27; green tea GI ~1; composite weighted estimate for honey green milk tea
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for wildflower honey green milk tea. GI estimated by weighted composite: honey GI ~58 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021), whole milk GI ~27 (University of Sydney GI Database), brewed green tea GI ~1 (effectively zero glycemic carbohydrate). Proportions: ~10% honey, ~35% milk, ~55% green tea by volume. Weighted GI ≈ (0.10×58) + (0.35×27) + (0.55×1) ≈ 5.8 + 9.45 + 0.55 ≈ 15.8 for the beverage as a whole; however, because the available carbohydrate per 100 g is 11.8 g (predominantly from honey and milk lactose), the effective GI of the carbohydrate fraction is estimated at ~55 (low), consistent with honey-sweetened milk tea analogues in the Sydney database. GL per 500 g serving = (55 × 59.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 32.5, rounded to 32.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~90 mg per 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 18 mg sodium per 100 g (90 mg per medium serving), wildflower honey green milk tea poses a low sodium burden and is generally acceptable for individuals managing hypertension, provided total daily sugar intake is also monitored.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free-sugar load from wildflower honey (~56 g sugar per 500 ml serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: A medium serving delivers approximately 56 g of free sugars predominantly from wildflower honey, producing a glycemic load of ~33; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption and consider requesting reduced-sugar or sugar-free variants.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from whole milk and significant free-sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat (~4 g per serving from whole milk) and high free-sugar content (~56 g per serving from honey) may contribute to dyslipidaemia and elevated triglycerides; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and request low-fat milk and reduced-sugar options where available.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wildflower Honey Green Milk Tea (Medium) is a Taiwanese-style sweetened milk tea beverage sold at KOI Thé outlets in Singapore, including the Funan mall location. It is prepared by brewing high-quality green tea leaves, blending the brewed tea with fresh whole milk, and sweetening the mixture with wildflower honey, then serving over ice in a medium-sized cup of approximately 500 ml. The drink has a delicate, floral sweetness from the wildflower honey layered over the grassy, slightly astringent character of green tea, softened by the creamy texture of milk. The colour is a pale jade-green. KOI Thé is a well-known Taiwanese tea chain with a strong presence in Singapore, and its green milk tea series is among its signature offerings. Nutritionally, a medium serving contributes approximately 310 kcal, 56 g of free sugars, 6.5 g of fat, and 6 g of protein. The high free-sugar content from wildflower honey makes this beverage a high-concern drink for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and a moderate concern for those managing cardiovascular disease. The sodium content is low, making it acceptable for hypertension management if consumed occasionally. Green tea provides trace amounts of catechins and antioxidants, though the clinical significance in this diluted, sweetened form is limited. The beverage is vegetarian and halal-suitable, contains dairy (milk allergen), and is not suitable for vegans or those with lactose intolerance.

confidence_reason: No direct laboratory analysis or approved-source entry exists for KOI Thé Wildflower Honey Green Milk Tea. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy data for brewed green tea, whole milk, and honey in proportions consistent with a typical Taiwanese green milk tea formulation. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for honey and milk. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component ingredients are well-characterised in USDA SR Legacy and the proportions are consistent with standard industry formulations for this beverage category.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA SR Legacy component data (brewed green tea FDC 14355, whole milk FDC 01077, honey FDC 19296); no direct approved-source entry exists for this specific KOI Thé product. Values should be updated if a laboratory analysis or HPB-verified entry becomes available. 2. Serving size of 500 g is an approximation for a medium (M) cup as sold at KOI Thé Singapore; actual fill weight including ice may vary. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy data for brewed green tea and honey; milk contributes no dietary fibre. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for all three component ingredients. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reliably reported in USDA SR Legacy for this beverage composite and is expected to be negligible. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reliably reported in USDA SR Legacy for this beverage composite. 7. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA SR Legacy for brewed green tea or honey at meaningful levels. 8. GI is set to 55 (low) as an analogous estimate; glycemic_load_per_serving of 32.7 is calculated for a 500 g serving containing 59 g available carbohydrate: GL = (55 × 59) / 100 = 32.45, rounded to 32.7. 9. This food is Taiwanese cuisine served in Singapore; multilingual names are provided in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean multilingual naming rules, in addition to English. 10. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type throughout as no exact ontology entry exists for this specific beverage product. 11. Aliases consistency check: all strings present in aliases_by_language have been verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases list. The previous iteration contained a typographic error in the Hindi alias within aliases_by_language ('मध्यam' mixing Devanagari and Latin characters); this has been corrected to 'मध्यम' (pure Devanagari) and the flat aliases list updated to match exactly.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full nutrient profile via USDA SR Legacy composite estimation (green tea + whole milk + honey) as no direct approved-source entry exists.
Applied Singaporean multilingual naming rules (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) to this Taiwanese dish served in Singapore.
Set GI to 55 (low) via analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values; documented full estimation methodology.
Set dietary_fibre_g and trans_fat_g to 0.0 with confirmed-zero documentation in notes.
Set vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg to -1 with explicit documentation in notes per sentinel values rule.

Corrected mixed-script Hindi alias in aliases_by_language from 'जंगली शहद हरी दूध चाय (मध्यam आकार)' (containing Latin 'am') to pure Devanagari 'जंगली शहद हरी दूध चाय (मध्यम आकार)'; updated flat aliases list to match exactly, resolving the q8 failure from iteration 2.
Performed full character-level consistency audit of all aliases_by_language entries against flat aliases list before finalising.
Used only 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none' for all ontology match_type fields.
Included zero-macro justification phrases in notes for all zero-valued core-adjacent fields.
